Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 1 Analyze the Request Input A Python dictionary representing a DECLARE model for a loan application process It includes various constraint types existence absence exactlyone init respondedexistence coexistence response precedence succession altresponse altprecedence altsuccession chainresponse chainprecedence chainsuccession noncoexistence nonsuccession nonchainsuccession Each constraint has specific activities involved Context Real-life meanings of activities A through H and Z are provided Task Analyze the model discuss how activitiesconstraints shape a real-life scenario explain compliancequalitysequencing describe the interplay of rules and speculate on business goalsregulatory requirements Target An LLM me acting as an analyst Tone Professional analytical insightful and structured

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: 2 Deconstruct the DECLARE Model Existence ReceiveApplication GatherAdditionalDocuments must happen Absence ProceedWithoutCompliance must never happen Exactly One PreliminaryCreditCheck happens exactly once Init ReceiveApplication starts the process Responded Existence If AssembleLoanOfferPackage happens QualityAssuranceReview must follow eventually Coexistence GatherAdditionalDocuments and AuthorizeContractTerms must both occur in the same case Response If ReceiveApplication happens PreliminaryCreditCheck must follow eventually Precedence QualityAssuranceReview must happen before AuthorizeContractTerms Succession PreliminaryCreditCheck must be immediately followed by GatherAdditionalDocuments

Name: Abductive Reasoning - IND
Text: Altprecedence NotifyCustomer target TransferFunds In DECLARE notation usually precedence A B means B before A So altprecedence NotifyCustomer TransferFunds means TransferFunds before NotifyCustomer This makes sense for a Funds Sent notification

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Altsuccession GatherAdditionalDocuments immediately followed by QualityAssuranceReview Chainresponse If AssembleLoanOfferPackage happens TransferFunds must follow eventually Chainprecedence AuthorizeContractTerms target PreliminaryCreditCheck means if Authorize happens CreditCheck must have happened immediately before Chainsuccession QualityAssuranceReview immediately followed by AssembleLoanOfferPackage

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: Noncoexistence TransferFunds and ReceiveApplication cannot happen in the same case Wait This is a huge red flag If ReceiveApplication is init and existence it must happen If TransferFunds and ReceiveApplication are in noncoexistence then TransferFunds can never happen This looks like a contradiction in the provided model data

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Self-Correction on noncoexistence I need to point this out as a potential logical inconsistency Hypothesis The prompt might contain a deliberate error to see if I catch it Better Hypothesis In the context of the prompt asking me to analyze it I should highlight this constraint implication If ReceiveApplication is mandatory TransferFunds is banned This breaks the loan process Decision I will analyze the model as presented but highlight this specific constraint as a critical logical conflict

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: 3 Structure the Response Introduction Acknowledge the models purpose Loan Approval Process and the DECLARE framework Activity Analysis Briefly map the codes A-H to their roles Constraint Analysis Group constraints by their function Mandatory Steps OrderingSequencing Prohibitions Quality Gates Critical Observation The noncoexistence issue Business Regulatory Implications Conclusion Summary of the models effectiveness and areas for refinement

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: 4 Drafting the Content The Flow Start with ReceiveApplication Then CreditCheck Then Gather Docs Then QA Then Authorize Then Offer Then Transfer Then Notify Constraint Deep Dive InitExistence Ensures every case starts with an application and docs are gathered Absence ProceedWithoutCompliance is the poison pill Exactly One PreliminaryCreditCheck Prevents double-dipping or skipping

Name: Hypothesis Generation - PE
Text: 5 Refining the Noncoexistence Interpretation If A is in the trace G cannot be This means No Loans Ever Get Funded I must point this out Its the most important analytical insight A model that forbids the final step from happening in the same case as the first step is a broken model Alternative Maybe the prompt implies these are types of constraints and I should analyze them as if they were valid noting the contradiction

Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Okay the flow seems to be 1 Receive A 2 Credit B 3 Gather C 4 QA E 5 Authorize F 6 Offer D 7 Transfer G 8 Notify H Lets check the constraints against this flow Succession B C matches Altsuccession C E matches Chainsuccession E D matches Chainresponse D G matches Altresponse G H matches Altprecedence H G matches Precedence E F matches Chainprecedence F B Wait Chainprecedence Authorize Credit implies Credit immediately before Authorize But Succession says Credit immediately before Gather This is a hard conflict Analysis This model contains multiple logical contradictions 1 noncoexistence Transfer Receive Transfer impossible 2 chainprecedence Authorize Credit contradicts Succession

Name: Validation - PE
Text: 7 Final Polish Ensure tone is professional Use formatting bolding lists for readability Self-Correction Make sure to clearly distinguish between the intent of the rules and the technical reality of the noncoexistence constraint Strategy I must point these out A good analysis doesnt just praise the model it validates it I will treat these as Areas for Model Refinement or Potential Logical Conflicts
